question.text: India's life expectancy at birth increased from 58 years in 1990 to approximately how many years by 2020, according to World Bank data cited in the NISM workbook?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** According to World Bank data cited in the NISM XVII workbook, India's life expectancy at birth increased from 58 years in 1990 to 69.8 years in 2020. **The Trap:** 72.3 and 75.0 are plausible but incorrect — they may reflect projections or data from other sources. The workbook specifically cites 69.8 years. **Cross-Exam Context:** Life expectancy data is specific to Series XVII. Other NISM exams do not typically cite this statistic. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Use this data point to illustrate why retirement planning horizons should assume 85-90 years of age, not just the average life expectancy — many will live well beyond 69.8 years.
